journal_entry.rationale: Recording utility expense incurred but not yet paid. Debit tuple (0,0,1,0,0,0) requires 1 expense increase line. Credit tuple (1,0,0,0,0,0) requires 1 liability increase line. The utility service has been consumed, creating an obligation to pay (accounts payable or utilities payable). No tax lines needed as this is an accrual of the base amount; any HST/GST would have been recorded when the bill was originally issued or will be recorded at payment.

output.reason: Accruing a utility bill means recognizing the utility expense that has been incurred but not yet paid. The debit side records an expense increase (utility expense). This is position c in the tuple.

output.reason: The transaction "Accrue $50 utility bill" means recognizing a utility expense that has been incurred but not yet paid. Under IFRS accrual accounting, this requires: (1) debiting utility expense (expense increase) and (2) crediting accounts payable or accrued liabilities (liability increase). The initial debit tuple (0,0,1,0,0,0) correctly shows one expense increase line. The credit tuple (1,0,0,0,0,0) correctly shows one liability increase line. Both sides are consistent and correct. No correction needed.
